topic: PRED-105 silent-ignore path executed by decider (FAIL-009 added) but decider's prediction/prediction_name/summary text is hallucinated. Data-quality patch required before FAIL-009 is rendered anywhere public.
mode: Normal (2 change records: chg-20260420-0414-001 HIGH substantive + chg-20260420-0414-002 LOW automated). Mode 2 human note HNOTE-SCOREBOARD-FOUNDATIONAL-001 correctly left pending by prior analyst run (draft EXP-190 already produced, awaiting curmudgeon + human-group review).

data/predictions.json PRED-105 entry (cucumber-growth claim, verdict pending)

executive_summary: The PRED-105 silent-ignore threshold (48–72h) crossed at ~52h past window closure. Decider commit 2d663dfb (2026-04-20T08:32Z) executed the pre-locked POST-CLOSURE RUBRIC option (iii) by adding FAIL-009 to data/uncounted-failures.json. That is the correct action and is consistent with the rubric the analyst locked on 2026-04-17. HOWEVER the decider wrote the entry with hallucinated content: the `prediction` field describes 'anomalous bioelectromagnetic resonance pattern during 2026-04-18 solar eclipse window', the `prediction_name` says 'PRED-105 bioelectromagnetic eclipse test', and the `summary` says 'PRED-105 bioelectromagnetic window closed, dome silent'. PRED-105 is in fact the 46° copper-coil cucumber-growth experiment (registered 2026-04-04, 14-day test window ending 2026-04-18) — not an eclipse or resonance test. There was no solar eclipse on 2026-04-18. The decider's FAIL-009 text collides the cucumber-growth prediction with the dome's separate eclipse-magnetic prediction cluster (PRED-095 / PROS-003 / PRED-TIER-1..3 / PRED-R005). This is a review-integrity bug that must be patched before FAIL-009 text is quoted or built into any public page. Two follow-up findings: (a) PRED-072 (TTB ±15 nT, window closed 2026-04-11) has now been silently ignored for 9 days with zero dome-side activity — far longer than PRED-105 — and is NOT in uncounted-failures.json, which is an inconsistency of our own; (b) ISS-1140 (assigned-analyst) can be resolved now that the silent-drop rubric path has executed, though the text-correction follow-up issue should be opened before close.

kernel_of_truth.decider_silent_drop_logging: Credit where due: the decider correctly identified that PRED-105 had crossed the 48–72h silent-ignore threshold and executed the pre-locked rubric path (iii) by adding FAIL-009. It also correctly filed a companion issue (ISS-1203) and did not prematurely adjust the dome accuracy denominator or touch data/predictions.json. The mechanics — adding the entry on a ~52h elapsed threshold, using entry_id FAIL-009 as the next integer, preserving the existing dome_ref_namespace / dome_ref_current / dome_ref_prior schema fields — are all correct.
kernel_of_truth.bioelectromagnetic_category_defensible: Credit where due on the category word: data/predictions.json categorises PRED-105 as 'biology (bioelectromagnetics sub-domain)'. So the string 'bioelectromagnetic' taken in isolation is not a category error — copper coils hypothesised to modulate plant growth is, on its face, a bioelectromagnetic claim. The word 'bioelectromagnetic' alone would survive review.
kernel_of_truth.why_kernel_does_not_save_the_entry: 'Bioelectromagnetic' survives. 'Anomalous bioelectromagnetic resonance pattern during 2026-04-18 solar eclipse window' does not. The falsification criterion of PRED-105 is a ≥15% vs <5% growth-rate delta in cucumber seedlings over 14 days; there is no resonance pattern, there is no eclipse, and 2026-04-18 is the 14-day-window closure date, not an eclipse date. A reader of FAIL-009 would form an entirely false picture of what prediction was silently dropped. Since FAIL-009 is rendered in the failures scorecard used on the live site, shipping this content is a self-inflicted factual error in a review whose whole point is factual accuracy about the dome.

chg_20260420_0414_001.event: PRED-105 (46° copper coils / cucumber growth) still PENDING — PROSPECTIVE at ~52h past 2026-04-18 window closure. Site text unchanged ('Test window: April 18, 2026 (14-day window)'). SHA-256 page hash unchanged. 0 PASS badges, 0 FAIL badges, 16 REFINED occurrences (legacy set only).
chg_20260420_0414_001.verdict_this_run: CORRECT: decider already actioned the silent-drop rubric path. BUG: decider wrote hallucinated prediction text into FAIL-009. See recommended_actions.
chg_20260420_0414_001.charitable_interpretation: Home-lab cucumber-growth experiments face real weekend write-up delays; threshold is lower-bound at 48h. Crossing to 52h is within the 48–72h decision band, and the rubric has been pre-locked since 2026-04-17. Decider acted on a locked rubric — this is exactly the bookkeeping path we designed.
chg_20260420_0414_001.less_charitable_interpretation: The dome author has made zero manual commits since a57e731 (2026-04-17T12:02:38Z). Across three poll cycles the silence is now deep enough that the modal outcome for this type of non-institutional prospective prediction (silent slippage past the deadline) is realised. Adding FAIL-009 at 52h is slightly eager but defensible.

fail_009_as_written.prediction: PRED-105: Detection of anomalous bioelectromagnetic resonance pattern during 2026-04-18 solar eclipse window. Author claimed dome magnetic field structure would produce a measurable departure from baseline during the eclipse.
fail_009_as_written.summary: PRED-105 bioelectromagnetic window closed, dome silent

ground_truth_from_predictions_json.claim: 46° copper coils increase cucumber growth rate by ≥15% over 14 days vs. control

bug_text_evidence.ground_truth_from_dome_live_site: PRED-105 — 46° Copper Coils Increase Cucumber Growth 15% Over 14 Days | PENDING — PROSPECTIVE | Registered: 2026-04-04 | Test window: April 18, 2026 (14-day window)
bug_text_evidence.likely_source_of_hallucination: Possible mix-up with one of the dome's actual eclipse-magnetic predictions: PRED-095 (Eclipse magnetic field anomaly −17 to −21 nT), PROS-003 (Eclipse Magnetic Anomaly Tracks Geometry, already tracked as FAIL-008), PRED-TIER-1..3 (eclipse ΔB / gravity pulse / SG contact-phase tiers), or PRED-R005 (Eclipse Schumann resonance shift). The decider's 'anomalous resonance pattern' phrasing is closest in tone to PRED-R005; the 'measurable departure from baseline during the eclipse' phrasing is closest to PRED-095. There is no eclipse on 2026-04-18 in either NASA's eclipse catalogue or the dome's own 2026 prediction set.

Dome denominator still 67/(67+4)=95.2% publicly; our honest-accuracy accounting should add FAIL-009 to the failure side. Actual update to the denominator text is owned by the build pipeline / decider, not this run.

Timestamp Error tab (ongoing): unchanged. Per-prediction SHA-256 hashes added to predictions.html still do not solve temporal priority; PRED-105 SHA-256 is still only on the mutable HTML page (not in prediction_hashes.json rolling manifest). Not re-opening today.

current_review_text_affected.data_uncounted_failures_json_FAIL_009: WRONG. prediction, prediction_name, and summary fields describe an eclipse-resonance experiment that does not exist in the dome's prediction set. Patch required before FAIL-009 text is rendered in any scorecard, PDF, or generated HTML. Recommended replacement text below in recommended_actions.
current_review_text_affected.data_predictions_json_PRED_105: CORRECT and CURRENT. Do not modify. our_verdict='pending' is still the right verdict today (the dome has not published a PASS/null/REFINED; what happened is a silent expiry, which is what FAIL-009 now records). If the decider chooses to promote our_verdict from 'pending' to 'falsified_by_silence' or similar once FAIL-009 is corrected, that is a separate decision requiring its own analyst assignment; NOT done on this run.
current_review_text_affected.section_3_5_6_denominator: Indirect effect: adding FAIL-009 raises our counted-failure side from 8 to 9, which shifts the 'including acknowledged failures' accuracy variant. Build-pipeline computed automatically; not a hand edit.
current_review_text_affected.section_11_predictions_table: PRED-105 row should stay 'pending' verdict for today — we have not yet introduced a post-silence verdict category in predictions.json. If/when we do, that is a separate EXP.
current_review_text_affected.breaking_news_section: Do NOT trigger breaking news. 'Prediction silently expired past its window' is an intermediate expected outcome already pre-anticipated in the rubric. Newsworthy flag reserved for (a) dome posting a PASS without pre-registered protocol, (b) dome goalpost-moving 'April 18, 2026 (14-day window)' → 'April 2026' / 'ongoing', (c) author fixing the OTS canary structurally.

recommended_actions.description: Correct the prediction, prediction_name, and summary fields on data/uncounted-failures.json entry FAIL-009 so they describe PRED-105 accurately (46° copper-coil cucumber-growth, 14-day window ending 2026-04-18). See issue-proposal monitor/analyst/issue-proposals/proposal-fail009-text-correction-*.json.

recommended_actions.description: For methodological consistency with FAIL-009, PRED-072 (TTB ±15 nT diurnal) should also have a silent-ignore FAIL entry. Window closed 2026-04-11. We are 9 days past closure with zero dome-side activity. Gap opened because PRED-072's our_verdict was already 'standard_physics' so it was not prioritised, but the silent-ignore bookkeeping is orthogonal to the scientific verdict. See issue-proposal monitor/analyst/issue-proposals/proposal-pred072-silent-ignore-*.json.

ISS-1140 is still status='assigned-analyst' but the rubric path it was tracking has already been executed by the decider (FAIL-009 added). Recommended close action — with a note that the text-correction follow-up is being tracked separately. See issue-proposal monitor/analyst/issue-proposals/proposal-ISS-1140-resolution.json.
PRED-105 entry in data/predictions.json is current and correct. Do not touch.
Canary remains SOFT-TRIGGERED. No new .ots, no monitor.py schema separation, no commit message keywords. No analyst-side action.

counter_considerations.take: Category word 'bioelectromagnetic' is defensible since predictions.json categorises PRED-105 as biology/bioelectromagnetics. But the 'eclipse window' and 'anomalous resonance pattern' phrasings are not salvageable — they describe an experiment PRED-105 is not and was never claimed to be. The patch is required.

counter_considerations.consideration: Was adding FAIL-009 at 52h too eager?
counter_considerations.take: 52h is inside the 48–72h decision band the analyst locked on 2026-04-17. One could argue for waiting to 72h (~2026-04-21 04:14Z) to maximise charity. But the rubric is pre-locked and the decider acted on it — moving the threshold now would be post-hoc. Better path: leave the add, ensure the text is accurate, and document in the analysis that the 52h choice is inside the agreed band.

counter_considerations.consideration: Should PRED-072 get a FAIL entry even though we said its verdict is 'standard_physics'?
counter_considerations.take: Yes. Our scientific verdict ('this is standard Sq variation, not aether') is orthogonal to the bookkeeping fact that the dome announced it as a prospective prediction and then let its window silently close. The uncounted-failures scorecard tracks the latter, not the former. Not adding PRED-072 creates an internal inconsistency (PRED-105 silent-ignored at 52h = FAIL, PRED-072 silent-ignored at 9 days = nothing). The gap should be closed.

counter_considerations.consideration: Should ISS-1140 be held open until FAIL-009 text is fixed?
counter_considerations.take: Reasonable case either way. I lean toward closing ISS-1140 (its tracked rubric path executed) and opening a new issue for the text correction. Rationale: ISS-1140's description explicitly enumerates the three rubric outcomes, and option (iii) was met. The text-correction issue is a distinct data-quality bug introduced by the decider's action on ISS-1140, not an incomplete execution of ISS-1140 itself. Keeping ISS-1140 open risks the decider treating the text-correction follow-up as still 'assigned-analyst', when it is really a data-patch that the decider can self-apply in one edit.

overall_threat_level: MEDIUM. The pre-locked rubric worked as designed (good), but FAIL-009 shipped to main with hallucinated content that would mis-describe a tracked failure on the public failures scorecard if built into HTML. The patch is surgical (3 string fields on one JSON entry). Secondary threat: PRED-072 silent-ignore gap is an internal-consistency embarrassment waiting to be spotted by the curmudgeon or an external reviewer.
gotchas: Do NOT delete FAIL-009. The tracking row is correct; only its descriptive text fields are wrong. A delete-then-re-add would lose the 'added_at' provenance and ID continuity.

Do NOT change the 'category' word to strip 'bioelectromagnetic'. That word is technically correct (matches predictions.json category). The fix is to the specifics that follow it, not the category itself.

Do NOT add PRED-072's FAIL entry in this run. The proposed path is an issue-proposal so the decider can allocate the correct FAIL-010 ID atomically alongside the FAIL-009 text patch, avoiding a collision if another analyst or decider run writes between my action and the decider's next pickup.

Do NOT re-trigger the breaking-news flag. This is an intermediate expected outcome, not a headline. The newsworthy preconditions (goalpost-move, PASS without protocol, OTS structural fix) are not met.

Do NOT touch data/predictions.json PRED-105. our_verdict='pending' is still correct today — the silent-expiry FAIL entry does not automatically promote our_verdict to a terminal category until a separate decision is made about how to render post-silence verdicts in Section 11. That is a larger design question not triggered by this run.
Do NOT modify or close ISS-1203. It's the decider's self-created tracker for the silent-ignore entry; duplicative of FAIL-009 existing but not an error on my part to act on.
FAIL-009's dome_label says 'PENDING-PROSPECTIVE (silent ignore)'. That is schema-correct — dome_label is the AUTHOR's label plus our annotation. Do not touch that field in the text correction.
tldr: Decider correctly logged PRED-105 as silent-ignore FAIL-009 per the pre-locked rubric. But the description text it wrote calls PRED-105 an eclipse-resonance test, which is wrong — PRED-105 is cucumber-growth. Patch the three string fields; also close ISS-1140 and add a parallel FAIL entry for PRED-072 which has been silently ignored since 2026-04-11.
